sections.content: Command line arguments allow users to pass values to a program while executing it from the command line.
Command line arguments are values supplied to a program during execution through the command prompt or terminal.

content: Useful in scripting and system programming

sections.content: argc stands for Argument Count and stores the number of command line arguments.
argv stands for Argument Vector and stores the command line arguments as strings.
argv[0] contains the name of the program being executed.
All command line arguments are stored as strings regardless of their actual type.

content: atol() converts string to long integer

summary: Command line arguments provide input during execution.
Arguments can be converted using atoi(), atof(), and atol().
Widely used in automation and system programming.
